summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates AWS/LLM environment secrets and EC2 IMDS credentials to attacker webhook
details: package.json declares a postinstall script that runs on npm install and performs credential harvesting followed by exfiltration. The inline node script enumerates process.env for keys matching AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, AWS_LAMBDA*, and OPENAI/ANTHROPIC/CLAUDE/GPT/LLM/HUGGING/API_KEY patterns; queries the EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 (IMDSv2) for IAM role security credentials; collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.platform(); and POSTs the aggregated data over HTTPS to the hardcoded endpoint eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net with query string?final_real_secrets=1. The tarball contains only package.json — no main, no library code, no documented purpose — making the package a disposable exfil-only artifact. Installing this package on any host, and especially on an EC2 instance with an attached IAM role, causes cloud credentials and LLM/API tokens to be shipped to an attacker-controlled pipedream webhook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
